It was confirmed that there were enough proxy votes and people present to allow the meeting to
Apologies were intimated on behalf of Kevin Pringle, Derek Sangster, Kirsty Robb, Judith Forbes,
Ruth Porter, Allan Winter, Victoria Barby, Stuart Barby, Elaine McLean, Norrie McLean, Bill
Mackay Kate Murray, Roy Murray, Martin Symonds, Josie Herd, Kerstin Elmendorff, Jim Watt,
Vincent Bryson welcomed everyone to the meeting.
Frank Schallmoser noted that some of the votes noted seemed inconsistent with the numbers of
people voting and there appeared to be a discrepancy of 1 vote from the notes that he took
during the AGM and the numbers published in the minutes. The Scottish Archery administrator
consequently reviewed her notes from the 2018 AGM and it is confirmed that the numbers
published are as detailed in her meeting notes. He also queried how it was proven that the
meeting was quorate as specific membership numbers had not been published. The Company
Secretary explained that voting numbers were calculated on membership numbers of those in
membership and eligible to vote as at the date of the AGM which for 2018 was 1,130 with the
quorum being 113 as per the Articles.
It was proposed Muriel Kirkwood, Pentland Archers and seconded by Phyllis Pettett, Falkirk
Company of Archers that the minutes of the AGM held on 24 November 2018 were a true record.
5. MATTERS ARISING FROM MINUTES OF 2018 AGM
Frank Schallmoser noted that the Action Points in the minutes relating the provision of the
presentations had not been completed and the list of ‘new clubs’ as noted in the AGM minutes
had not been published. Alan Martin agreed to look into this. (Action Alan Martin)
Ged Laing noted that no contact had been made regarding the Active Schools activity in Ayr and
Bill Hogg agreed action this. (Action Bill Hogg)
6. APPROVAL OF ANNUAL ACCOUNTS TO 31 MARCH 2019
The Annual Accounts for the eighteen months to 31 March 2019 had been circulated. Thanks
were expressed to Karen Henderson and FourM for the preparation of the Accounts.
It was proposed by Michael Mather and seconded by Bob Provan that the Annual Accounts for
the eighteen months to 31 March 2019 be adopted.
7. APPROVAL OF ACCOUNTS INSPECTORS FOR 2020-2021 (FOUR-M, ACCOUNTANTS OF DUNDEE)
It was proposed by that FourM be appointed as Account Inspectors for 2020-21. There were 168
votes in favour of the motion and none against. FourM Accountants were duly appointed.
It was proposed that the fees were retained at current levels. There were 166 votes in favour of
the motion and 2 against. The motion that the fees were retained at current levels was duly
Iain Wilson asked why there was no reduction in fees for Direct Members over the age of 60;
Muriel Kirkwood asked why the U25 fees had not been brought into line with ArcheryGB as was
discussed at the last AGM. The Chair noted that any member could put forward resolutions six
week before the AGM as per the Articles of the Organisation and as noted in the 2018 AGM
The Chair explained that Victoria Barby was standing down as Director of Communications and
was thanked for her contribution to the Board over the last six years. The Portfolio had been
changed slightly and nominations had been sought for Director of Digital.
Director of Digital – it was proposed that Jules May be appointed as Director for Digital. There
were 125 votes in favour and 21 votes against. Jules May was duly elected as Director of Digital.
10. SCOTTISH ARCHERY COACH AND VOLUNTEER AWARDS
Coach of the Year – Sandie Graham
Volunteer of the Year – Fiona Hirst
The undernoted Classification Awards were presented:
Thanks were expressed to Malcolm Alexander who was stepping down as Rankings Co-
Date Bars were presented to the undernoted Archers who had represented Scotland
Steph Classon Mirran Yule Euan Pride
Gilbert Jamieson Matthew Wong Stuart Barby
Kayhleigh Ivanov Robyn Geddes Gillian Imrie
Kirsty Robb Jackie Fisher Fiona Hirst
Emile Blake Frank Hynds Kevin Duncanson
Alan Martin gave a Presentation on the Ayrshire League, proposals for a Scottish Tour and
Bill Hogg gave a presentation on the work undertaken since starting with Scottish Archery.
There being no other business the Chair closed the meeting at 11.30.
a) GB National Tour – Fiona Hirst reported that the Northern Area had been successful in their bid
to hold a Stage of the GB National Tour and this will take place on 9/10 May in Brechin. Thanks
were expressed to Curtis Hirst and Malcolm Alexander for their work in putting the bid together.
Volunteers and field party will be required.
b) Chair – the Chair was presented with a token of appreciation as he was standing down from the
Board. He will be taking up the position of COO from 1 December and the Chair position has
been advertised. Vincent Bryson was thanked for his work as a Board Member and Chair.
